feed.title: Control and Automation for Intelligent Systems Lab
feed.subtitle: Research in the Control and Automation for Intelligent Systems Lab focuses on developing, analyzing, and applying control theory and artificial intelligence to improve the safety, flexibility, and performance of complex, dynamic systems, such as manufacturing systems.

entry.title: Automate 2026
entry.content: CAIS lab attended the Automate 2026 in Chicago, Illinois, USA. They showcased some of the recent research with robotic assembly and had great interactions with the robotics and automation community.
entry.summary: CAIS lab attended the Automate 2026 in Chicago, Illinois, USA. They showcased some of the recent research with robotic assembly and had great interactions with the robotics and automation community.

entry.title: ASME Manufacturing Science and Engineering Conference (MSEC 2026)

link.title: ASME Manufacturing Science and Engineering Conference (MSEC 2026)

entry.content:
Ilya served on the organizing committee and co-chaired this amazing conference, while Hongliang helped organize the tutorial: Robotic Systems for Enabling Flexible and Intelligent Manufacturing.

Yifei and Jiabao presented their research at the ASME Manufacturing Science and Engineering Conference (MSEC 2026) in State College, USA.

Read more here!
entry.summary: Ilya served on the organizing committee and co-chaired this amazing conference, while Hongliang helped organize the tutorial: Robotic Systems for Enabling Flexible and Intelligent Manufacturing.

entry.title: Mostafa’s Ph.D. Defense
entry.content:
A big congratulations to Mostafa on passing his ME Ph.D. defense! This achievement marks the culmination of years of hard work and commitment.

Best wishes to him as he moves forward to new opportunities and exciting challenges!
entry.summary: A big congratulations to Mostafa on passing his ME Ph.D. defense! This achievement marks the culmination of years of hard work and commitment.

entry.title: 18th Workshop on Discrete Event Systems
entry.content:
CAIS Lab Presents Three Papers on Robustness, Verification, and Resilient Control

CAIS Lab members presented three recent papers on formal methods, cyber-physical systems, and intelligent manufacturing systems.

Jonghan Lim presented “Logic-Based Verification of Task Allocation for LLM-Enabled Multi-Agent Manufacturing Systems,” co-authored by Jonghan Lim, Mostafa Tavakkoli Anbarani, Rômulo Meira-Góes, and Ilya Kovalenko.

Ilya Kovalenko presented “Attack-Resilient Control of Finite-State Cyber-Physical Systems via Inspection States,” co-authored by Mostafa Tavakkoli Anbarani, Jonghan Lim, Rômulo Meira-Góes, and Ilya Kovalenko.

Shih-Jie Shih presented “NeuroSymbolic Robustness Analysis for Discrete Systems with Respect to Transition Deviations,” co-authored by Shih-Jie Shih, Jonghan Lim, Ilya Kovalenko, and Rômulo Meira-Góes.

Congratulations to all authors on sharing this work with the research community!
entry.summary: CAIS Lab Presents Three Papers on Robustness, Verification, and Resilient Control

entry.title: Multi-lab hangout
entry.content: Multi-lab hangout! Very exciting games with Dr. Derek Hall’s and Dr. Sam Grauer’s labs.
entry.summary: Multi-lab hangout! Very exciting games with Dr. Derek Hall’s and Dr. Sam Grauer’s labs.

entry.title: Welcome New Undergraduate Researcher
entry.content: Welcome to Sihan Wang, who joined the lab as an undergraduate researcher!
entry.summary: Welcome to Sihan Wang, who joined the lab as an undergraduate researcher!

entry.title: 2026 Built Environment Showcase
entry.content:
Ilya and Hongliang attended the 2026 Built Environment Showcase at Penn State, an interactive event that invites our community to engage with innovators, ask questions, and explore what’s next.

Congratulations, Hongliang! His poster, “Hierarchical System-Level Energy Efficiency Digital Twin Framework for Energy-Aware Production Scheduling: Application to a Beverage Bottling Plant,” was presented in the Student Research Poster Showcase.
entry.summary: Ilya and Hongliang attended the 2026 Built Environment Showcase at Penn State, an interactive event that invites our community to engage with innovators, ask questions, and explore what’s next.

entry.title: Jonghan’s Ph.D. Scholarship
entry.content: Congratulations to Jonghan on receiving the IME Department Spring 26 Marcus-Niebel-Schall Graduate Student Scholarship!
entry.summary: Congratulations to Jonghan on receiving the IME Department Spring 26 Marcus-Niebel-Schall Graduate Student Scholarship!

entry.title: Penn State News for Digital Twins Hub Research and Mentorship

link.title: Penn State News for Digital Twins Hub Research and Mentorship

entry.content:
Dr. Kovalenko, one of the faculty participants in the ICDS Digital Twins Hub’s Faculty-in-Residence Program, and Ph.D. candidate Hongliang Li were featured in a recent Penn State News article.

The project aims to develop a digital twin framework for more robust and predictive process control in water treatment systems.

Read more at the Digital Twins Hub’s Faculty-in-Residence Program highlights mentorship, research.
entry.summary: Dr. Kovalenko, one of the faculty participants in the ICDS Digital Twins Hub’s Faculty-in-Residence Program, and Ph.D. candidate Hongliang Li were featured in a recent Penn State News article.

entry.title: Seminar at University of Iowa
entry.content:
Dr. Kovalenko delivered the seminar “Developing Intelligent Automation for Smart and Sustainable Manufacturing Systems” at the University of Iowa.

Read more at the Developing Intelligent Automation and Robotics for Smart Manufacturing Systems.
entry.summary: Dr. Kovalenko delivered the seminar “Developing Intelligent Automation for Smart and Sustainable Manufacturing Systems” at the University of Iowa.
